topic: Decision Making to Improve Marketing Performance
subject: business-studies

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.

items.q: What three Ps are added to make the extended marketing mix (7Ps)?
items.correct: People (staff/service quality), Process (how the service is delivered), and Physical Evidence (tangible cues — uniforms, packaging, branding) — added to make the 4Ps relevant to services marketing.
items.misconception: The 7Ps are just the 4Ps repeated with different words.
items.random_wrong: The 7Ps include Profit, Planning and Performance.
items.why: The extended mix specifically addresses services, where customer experience depends on staff, process and atmospherics — not just product. Students treat 4Ps and 7Ps as interchangeable, missing what services marketing requires.

items.q: What did Mayo's Hawthorne studies suggest about motivation?
items.correct: Workers are motivated by social factors and the feeling of being noticed/valued — not just pay or physical conditions. Productivity rose because researchers paid attention to workers, not because lighting improved.
items.misconception: Mayo proved that better lighting increases productivity.
items.why: The Hawthorne effect is famously about attention and social meaning, not the experimental variable itself. Students remember the lighting experiment and miss the human relations conclusion Mayo drew from it.

items.q: Modigliani and Miller's irrelevance theorem was published in:
items.why: It's 1958. “1948” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Cost of capital. “Annual cost calculation” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Modigliani and Miller publish their capital structure theorem?
items.why: The real figure is 1958 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
